2603.27253.title: Mitigating Hallucination on Hallucination in RAG via Ensemble Voting
2603.27253.paper_summary: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) aims to reduce hallucinations in Large Language Models (LLMs) by integrating external knowledge. However, RAG introduces a critical challenge: hallucination on hallucination," where flawed retrieval results mislead the generation model, leading to compounded hallucinations. To address this issue, we propose VOTE-RAG, a novel, training-free framework with a two-stage structure and efficient, parallelizable voting mechanisms. VOTE-RAG includes: (1) Retrieval Voting, where multiple agents generate diverse queries in parallel and aggregate all retrieved documents; (2) Response Voting, where multiple agents independently generate answers based on the aggregated documents, with the final output determined by majority vote. We conduct comparative experiments on six benchmark datasets. Our results show that VOTE-RAG achieves performance comparable to or surpassing more complex frameworks. Additionally, VOTE-RAG features a simpler architecture, is fully parallelizable, and avoids the problem drift" risk. Our work demonstrates that simple, reliable ensemble voting is a superior and more efficient method for mitigating RAG hallucinations.
2603.27253.paper_summary_zh: 檢索增強生成（RAG）旨在通過整合外部知識來減少大型語言模型（LLMs）中的幻覺。然而，RAG 引入了一個關鍵挑戰：「幻覺上的幻覺」，在這種情況下，錯誤的檢索結果誤導生成模型，導致複合幻覺。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了 VOTE-RAG，一個新穎的無需訓練的框架，具有兩階段結構和高效的可並行投票機制。VOTE-RAG 包括：（1）檢索投票，其中多個代理並行生成多樣的查詢並聚合所有檢索到的文檔；（2）回應投票，其中多個代理根據聚合的文檔獨立生成答案，最終輸出由多數票決定。我們在六個基準數據集上進行了比較實驗。我們的結果顯示，VOTE-RAG 的性能與更複雜的框架相當或超過。此外，VOTE-RAG 具有更簡單的架構，完全可並行化，並避免了「問題漂移」的風險。我們的工作表明，簡單、可靠的集成投票是一種更優越且更高效的減輕 RAG 幻覺的方法。
2603.27253.author: Zequn Xie et.al.
